---
title: "Language reference map"
description: "A compact map to normative GenesisCode language semantics."
---

# Semantic map

| Area | Normative source | Key contract |
|---|---|---|
| Reader, printer, canonical hash | [CoreForm canon/hash](../docs/spec/COREFORM_CANON_HASH.md) | Equivalent forms print and hash identically |
| Evaluation and module scope | [Module scope](../docs/spec/MODULE_SCOPE.md) | Pure lexical evaluation; top-level recursive `def` |
| Runtime values and effect hashes | [Value/effect hash](../docs/spec/VALUE_EFFECT_HASH.md) | Total, stable hashes for replay |
| Seals and dispatch | [Seals/dispatch/replay](../docs/spec/SEALS_DISPATCH_REPLAY.md) | Protocol values cannot be forged |
| Types | [Types](../docs/spec/TYPES.md) | Typechecking remains outside TCB-A |
| Standard library | [Foundation stdlib](../docs/FOUNDATION_STDLIB_v0.2.md) | Behavior in libraries, not kernel forms |
| Effects and concurrency | [Concurrency](../docs/spec/CONCURRENCY_v0.1.md) | Deterministic scheduling facts and explicit host work |
| Optimization | [Optimizer](../docs/spec/OPTIMIZER.md) | Semantics preserved by validation |
| Translation tiers | [Translation validation](../docs/spec/TRANSLATION_VALIDATION.md) | Same canonical behavior across tiers |

## Exact inventory

The generated [symbol reference](symbols.qmd) is the closed, searchable inventory for the current agent profile. Unsupported classes are explicit; absence is not permission to invent syntax or behavior.
